untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of specific divergence types (6, 7, 1, 12) between price action and a companion indicator series from 38 possible options. This requires: (1) divergence pattern recognition logic not expressible as simple indicator comparisons, (2) dynamic selection from 38 companion series based on confirmation rules, (3) channel normalization across 15-day lookback with threshold displacement calculations. These are complex multi-step algorithmic detections that cannot be reduced to atomic indicator conditions using the allowed operators and indicator set.
translation_notes: The ARONSON_DRY_0_2 signal is fundamentally a divergence detection algorithm requiring pattern analysis and multi-series correlation logic. While the threshold displacement (10 points) and channel lookback (15 days) are numeric parameters that could be configured, the core entry condition—identifying one of 4 specific divergence types across 38 possible companion series—requires custom divergence detection logic unavailable in the standard indicator set. A backtest engine would need a dedicated divergence detection module or this signal should be implemented as a custom Python/algorithmic rule rather than a structured condition tree.
